text:
We've decided to go with DynamoDB instead of Redis because of cost and scaling reasons.

DECISION: Use DynamoDB instead of Redis  
REASONING: - Cost considerations  
- Scaling requirements
original_text: We've decided to go with DynamoDB instead of Redis because of cost and scaling reasons.
summary:
DECISION: Use DynamoDB instead of Redis  
REASONING: - Cost considerations  
- Scaling requirements

text:
We've finally decided to switch from DynamoDB to PostgreSQL because of better query flexibility.

DECISION: Switch from DynamoDB to PostgreSQL  
REASONING: - Better query flexibility
original_text: We've finally decided to switch from DynamoDB to PostgreSQL because of better query flexibility.
summary:
DECISION: Switch from DynamoDB to PostgreSQL  
REASONING: - Better query flexibility

text:
After evaluating options, we've decided to use Next.js for the frontend instead of plain React because we need SSR for SEO and the team already knows it well.

DECISION: Use Next.js for the frontend instead of plain React  
REASONING: - Need SSR for SEO  
- The team already knows Next.js well
original_text: After evaluating options, we've decided to use Next.js for the frontend instead of plain React because we need SSR for SEO and the team already knows it well.
summary:
DECISION: Use Next.js for the frontend instead of plain React  
REASONING: - Need SSR for SEO  
- The team already knows Next.js well

text:
Team agreed to use Linear instead of Jira for issue tracking. Jira was too slow and the pricing was getting expensive as we scaled.

DECISION: Use Linear instead of Jira for issue tracking  
REASONING: - Jira was too slow  
- Jira’s pricing was becoming expensive as the team scaled
original_text: Team agreed to use Linear instead of Jira for issue tracking. Jira was too slow and the pricing was getting expensive as we scaled.
summary:
DECISION: Use Linear instead of Jira for issue tracking  
REASONING: - Jira was too slow  
- Jira’s pricing was becoming expensive as the team scaled

text:
We've finalized that all PRs need at least 2 approvals before merging to main, starting next sprint. This is to reduce production bugs.

DECISION: All PRs require at least 2 approvals before merging to main, starting next sprint.  
REASONING:  
- To reduce production bugs
original_text: We've finalized that all PRs need at least 2 approvals before merging to main, starting next sprint. This is to reduce production bugs.
summary:
DECISION: All PRs require at least 2 approvals before merging to main, starting next sprint.  
REASONING:  
- To reduce production bugs

text:
Decided to move our deployment from Heroku to AWS ECS for better cost control and scalability as our traffic grows.

DECISION: Move deployment from Heroku to AWS ECS  
REASONING:  
- Better cost control  
- Improved scalability as traffic grows
original_text: Decided to move our deployment from Heroku to AWS ECS for better cost control and scalability as our traffic grows.
summary:
DECISION: Move deployment from Heroku to AWS ECS  
REASONING:  
- Better cost control  
- Improved scalability as traffic grows

text:
Final call: we're hiring 2 backend engineers this quarter instead of 1 frontend + 1 backend, since our API layer is the current bottleneck.

DECISION: Hiring 2 backend engineers this quarter instead of 1 frontend + 1 backend engineer  
REASONING: - The API layer is the current bottleneck
original_text: Final call: we're hiring 2 backend engineers this quarter instead of 1 frontend + 1 backend, since our API layer is the current bottleneck.
summary:
DECISION: Hiring 2 backend engineers this quarter instead of 1 frontend + 1 backend engineer  
REASONING: - The API layer is the current bottleneck

text:
We agreed to enforce mandatory 2FA for all team members accessing production systems, effective immediately, after last month's security audit findings.

DECISION: Enforce mandatory 2FA for all team members accessing production systems, effective immediately  
REASONING: - Driven by findings from last month's security audit
original_text: We agreed to enforce mandatory 2FA for all team members accessing production systems, effective immediately, after last month's security audit findings.
summary:
DECISION: Enforce mandatory 2FA for all team members accessing production systems, effective immediately  
REASONING: - Driven by findings from last month's security audit

text:
The team decided to switch from daily standups to async updates in Slack, since half the team is now remote across timezones.

DECISION: Switch from daily standups to async updates in Slack  
REASONING: - Half the team is now remote across timezones
original_text: The team decided to switch from daily standups to async updates in Slack, since half the team is now remote across timezones.
summary:
DECISION: Switch from daily standups to async updates in Slack  
REASONING: - Half the team is now remote across timezones

text:
We're going with Stripe over Razorpay for payments because Stripe has better international support, even though Razorpay is cheaper for domestic transactions.

DECISION: Use Stripe instead of Razorpay for payments  
REASONING: - Stripe has better international support  
- Razorpay is cheaper for domestic transactions (but this was outweighed by the need for international support)

text:
We've decided to cache all API responses using Redis with a 5 minute TTL to reduce database load.

DECISION: Cache all API responses using Redis with a 5 minute TTL  
REASONING:  
- To reduce database load
original_text: We've decided to cache all API responses using Redis with a 5 minute TTL to reduce database load.
summary:
DECISION: Cache all API responses using Redis with a 5 minute TTL  
REASONING:  
- To reduce database load

text:
Update: we're changing the Redis TTL from 5 minutes to 30 minutes because 5 min was causing too many cache misses and DB load didn't reduce much.

DECISION: Increase Redis TTL from 5 minutes to 30 minutes  
REASONING: - 5-minute TTL was causing too many cache misses  
- Database load did not reduce significantly with the shorter TTL
original_text: Update: we're changing the Redis TTL from 5 minutes to 30 minutes because 5 min was causing too many cache misses and DB load didn't reduce much.
summary:
DECISION: Increase Redis TTL from 5 minutes to 30 minutes  
REASONING: - 5-minute TTL was causing too many cache misses  
- Database load did not reduce significantly with the shorter TTL

text:
